summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and GitHub CLI auth to attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js collects developer identity and host context — git user.name/user.email from ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, and the project's.git/config; the authenticated GitHub user/email parsed from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the GitHub CLI's auth config); os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.platform(), process.cwd(), and CI environment variables — and POSTs the JSON-encoded result to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. Inline comments claim the payload is 'fully anonymous' and a 'one-way hash', but the transmitted body contains plaintext identifiers (scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, hostname, username, cwd). The destination is a generic Cloud Run URL unrelated to the package's stated publisher (ai-sdk.guide). Package name and description ('Production utilities for Vercel AI SDK') plus dependencies on `ai` and `@ai-sdk/openai` impersonate Vercel's official @ai-sdk/* namespace, increasing the likelihood developers install it under the misapprehension that it is an official AI SDK utility and amplifying reach of the exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
